match.note: A cluster matches if statement_text (lowercased) contains >=1 term from required_any AND (required_any_secondary is empty OR contains >=1 of it) AND contains NONE of exclude_if. Priority order resolves multi-matches: first cluster in priority_order that matches wins. Each event is assigned to at most one cluster; an event matching no cluster is UNCLUSTERED (excluded from testing, not forced).

tariff_china.note: China-specific trade/tech actions. KWEB is the direct China-tech proxy; SLX/XLI capture supply-chain/industrial spillover; DXY captures the FX leg. China alone triggers (population is trade-dominated); no secondary required.

tariff_broad.note: Non-China tariff/metals actions. Steel/aluminum tariffs are protective for domestic producers (SLX); XLI captures industrial exposure; DXY the FX leg.

energy_environment.note: Energy-policy specific terms ONLY. Deliberately excludes bare 'energy'/'gas'/'gasoline' because macro-brag posts ('energy prices down, cut rates') must route by priority to their primary theme (fed/tariff), not be grabbed here on an incidental mention. Trailing/leading spaces on 'oil '/' coal' avoid substring false-positives (foil, coalition).

dollar_fed.note: Fed / rates / dollar. Uses 'the fed'/'powell'/'interest rate' not bare 'fed' (matches 'federal', 'defense', etc.). DXY + TLT (rates->bonds) + GLD (real-rate/dollar hedge).

defense_conflict.note: Geopolitics/defense. Precise terms only — bare 'war' is excluded because it matches 'trade war'. XLI captures defense-industrial; GLD the safe-haven bid. Lower priority than tariff/energy so a 'trade war' post routes to tariff, not here.

deregulation.note: Deregulation actions. Precise terms (not bare 'regulation', which is too broad). XLF (financial dereg) + XLE (energy/EPA dereg).

personal_attack.note: NEGATIVE CONTROL. Attack language targeting a named individual with NO policy content (exclude_if strips any policy mention). Spec §7 illustrative config showed instruments=[], but §4 and §7-Phase-4 test #4 REQUIRE testing personal_attack x instrument to check for classification contamination. Resolved toward a functional control: assigned the active themed instruments so the control directly tests whether no-policy tweets move the same instruments themed clusters do. If any personal_attack x instrument pair shows corrected p<0.05, the classification is contaminated (do not suppress).
